title: mandevilla tomentosa var. hispida
intro: Mandevilla tomentosa var. hispida, also known as the hairy mandevilla, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, derived from its alkaloid compounds. The health benefits associated with Mandevilla tomentosa var. hispida include relief from fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as wound healing and antimicrobial effects. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
